place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Bishan MRT station in the Circle line it is Station Number 15.
It is connecting one side with Harbour Front and another side with Marina Bay.
Same MRT connected with North South Line as Station Number 17.
It is getting connected with one side Jurang East another side with Marina South Pier.
It is well maintained. It is very neat and clean.
There are clear  signs for each and every directions.
No question if confusion.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
---

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Bishan MRT Station is one of the most convenient and well‑connected stations in Singapore. Serving both the North–South Line and Circle Line, it makes transfers quick and easy. The station is spacious, clean, and well‑maintained, with clear signage that helps commuters navigate smoothly. It connects directly to Junction 8 mall and the bus interchange, adding to its convenience. Facilities such as lifts, escalators, and ticketing machines are in good working order, and staff are approachable when assistance is needed. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall experience is reliable. A dependable hub for daily commuting and shopping.

---

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The base of my reviewing operations.

The station is usually well maintained and kept clean by the staff. During peak hours can get a bit squeezy but usually got enough space for everyone. Must give credits to the station staff here though, they're always willing to help out and maintain order in the station after big events where everyone is rushing to change lines.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
During peak period, I felt very unwell and stopped at one of the benches to rest. One of the staff noticed that I was feeling unwell. She prompted other staff members and even got a wheelchair after I communicated that I felt too ill to move to the sick bay. I was then moved to the sick bay area and someone accompanied me throughout in case I needed anything.

AKA Staff were super vigilant and friendly when I fell ill despite it being peak period

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
good layout of the station, but be prepared for extra large crowds during peak periods such as the mornings and evenings. Expect to wait for multiple trains during super crowded timings to even board the train.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast area and wanted to share my thoughts on East Coast Park. It remains one of the best-maintained spaces in Singapore, and its extensive connectivity to other parks makes it an ideal destination for long walks or cycling trips. I particularly enjoyed the ability to ride my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay via the park connector; it was always such a calm and refreshing experience.

The park offers a wonderful atmosphere with its blend of lush greenery and sea views. It is exceptionally well-equipped for families and fitness enthusiasts, featuring dedicated exercise stations, children’s play areas, and wide, separate paths for cycling and walking that prevent the space from feeling crowded.

Beyond the scenery, the facilities are excellent. There are convenient bicycle rentals, a variety of dining options in the restaurant area where you can enjoy the sea breeze, and well-maintained BBQ pits and shelters. For those interested in staying longer, the designated camping areas are a great touch, and I’ve always found the washrooms and bathing facilities to be very clean.

Overall, it is a perfect place to relax, exercise, and enjoy nature, whether you are visiting alone or with friends and family.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
East Coast Park is one of the best and most well-maintained parks in Singapore. It is a very big park and is beautifully connected to other parks across Singapore, making it perfect for long walks or cycling trips. The park has lots of nice scenery, with greenery and sea views that make the atmosphere very relaxing. In some areas, there are exercise equipment stations and children’s play areas, which are great for families and fitness lovers.

There is a dedicated cycling path and a separate walking path, both of which are wide and well maintained, so it never feels crowded. You can also rent bicycles, and there are fun activities and attractions that bring in many people. The park has a restaurant area where you can enjoy a variety of foods while feeling the cool sea breeze. You can book BBQ pits, and there are huts available for shelter in case of rain. There are also designated camping areas for those who want to stay overnight.

I used to live near the East Coast area, and what I loved most was riding my bicycle all the way to Gardens by the Bay, as the park connector makes this possible. It was always a calm and refreshing experience. On top of that, the washrooms and bathing areas are very clean and well maintained. Overall, East Coast Park is a perfect place to relax, exercise, eat, and enjoy nature, whether alone, with friends, or with family.

review_text: Nice place to walk, enjoy the sea breeze. Or one can enjoy coffee/drinks at one of the restaurant or cafe or even hawker centre. Cycling or inline blading can let u exercise and head to less crowded spot on the further end of the beach.

This is one of the most beautiful parks I’ve ever visited in Singapore.

It has plenty of trees, clean walking paths, and comfortable benches where people can relax and enjoy the peaceful atmosphere. I especially recommend visiting in the morning because the weather is cooler and the park feels very refreshing

There are also many trash bins around the park, so it is convenient for visitors who bring drinks or snacks. The area is well maintained, clean, and perfect for taking a walk, reading a book, or simply spending quiet time away from the busy city. 🌳

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
AMK Hub is one of the most convenient and practical malls in Ang Mo Kio. The location is excellent, as it is directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT station and the bus interchange, making it very easy to reach by public transport.

The mall has a good mix of shops, dining options, supermarkets, daily essentials, and services. It is suitable for families, students, office workers, and residents living nearby. Whether you are looking for groceries, a quick meal, shopping, banking services, or just a place to walk around, AMK Hub has almost everything you need in one location.

I like that the mall is very accessible and easy to navigate. There are many food choices, from fast food and takeaway options to cafés and restaurants for proper meals. It is also a convenient place to run errands, meet friends, or spend time with family.

Although the mall can get quite crowded during weekends and meal times, the overall environment is still lively, clean, and well maintained. The air-conditioning is comfortable, and the shops are arranged in a way that makes it easy to move around.

Overall, AMK Hub is a reliable and convenient neighbourhood mall with a good variety of food, shopping, and essential services. It is definitely one of the best places to visit in Ang Mo Kio for everyday needs, meals, and casual shopping.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: AMK Hub is a really convenient neighbourhood mall, especially since it’s directly connected to Ang Mo Kio MRT and the bus interchange. It’s super easy to get to, which makes it a popular stop for quick meals or shopping. There’s a wide range of food options here—from food courts with local and international dishes to plenty of fast-food and restaurant chains. It’s not as exciting as the bigger malls, but it’s great for everyday needs and casual dining.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A good, classic neighbourhood mall with food options that leave you spoilt for choice. A rectangular-shaped mall with basements mostly eateries, Level 1 & 2 mostly shops, and Level 3 is mostly educational centres.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Went to AMK Hub for lunch. ate at that Malaysia Boleh. There are many varieties of food here suitable for your lunch or dinner...My hubby always eat the cray pot rice which comes with chicken and salted fish. That day we ate that fish ball black noodles. This store add some tasty dark sauce to their noodles which gives a different taste to it's noodles. Taste very yummy. I had their fried tang hoon. Same store...They have varies type of fried noodles which you must tried. Not bad too. We always end our meal with a bowl of Chendol which is classic with all Malaysia Boleh outlets...They definitely have this menu in all their outlets without fail...AMK Hub is filled with varieties type of food on their 3 floors esp basement 1 and basement 2...a full line of snacks and food for you to choose from. Try coming here for your meals...no regret....I have had enclosed photos of our meals that day...Enjoy!!!!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Lots of shops and less shopping shops.only essential shops are available like daily use stuffs. More favorite thing attracted me it the play area next to jollie bee food court

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Had Supper here for the very first time. Loved everything about this place. The atmosphere, the music, the food, Chef Badrul and I assume there is another Chef as well, nailed it on the food we ordered, especially since we asked for the pasta to be spicy. And the portions are really generous. Thank you Chef Badrul and Chef.

Shahe was very welcoming from the start, made sure we had all we needed, and giving us chili flakes without any request, was a really nice touch. Also, he saw us taking selfies and offered to help us take photos. Really great service here. Also, as a bartender, the drink, PassionFruit Mango Earl Grey Tea, was off the roof! Thank you Shahe.

Joyce took over, and we ordered a little too much and she was really nice to help pack our food, even suggested separating certain things. Checking on all our food and overall dining experience. Thank you Joyce.

I love the attentiveness of the team. Overall really satisfied. I would definitely come back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Dropped by here for a quick lunch on a weekdays. Still have seats but I still highly recommend to reserve the seats via Google. The food portion is great and we literally can share one course! The weekday lunch is affordable. The roasted garlic is chef kiss. Thank you for this amazing place!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Food was absolutely delicious and flavourful. The seafood aglio olio and Turkey bacon chicken ham pasta was delicious.

Their iced teas were amazing as well with a unique touch and ingredients to it.

The ambience and overall atmosphere of the restaurant was very good and lively. Staffs were very friendly and attentive to our needs.

Overall a very good experience and highly recommended place for family and friends

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This place is worth the hype! Found them through TikTok while searching for a place to celebrate my friend's birthday! The staff are really nice! And were really cute playing the birthday song and providing candles and lighter for the cake!

I recommend their carbonara turkey bacon and tomato vegan pasta! Both dishes surprised me and my friend with their quality!! Not too heavy on the cream for the carbonara, and for the vegan pasta was one of the best vegan version I've tried. For drinks, their premium drinks are really nice especially the strawberry coconut, it has such a smooth feeling and the coconuts really complements the strawberry Earl grey!!

We'll be coming back!!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
This is a very pretty cafe near the Bishan Sports Complex. The exterior is decorated with a mini camping feel and the interior is resort-like. You can sit on the camping chairs while waiting for that cup of coffee or food.

Food wise, portions are good for the price paid. We ordered the
. Lamb Chops $24.90
. King Prawns with Tom Yum Pasta $20.90
. Fried Chicken & Waffle $16.90
. Prime Cheese Burger$16.90
. House Salad $9.90
.
The Fried Chicken & Waffle and Tom Yum Pasta were our favourites, especially the pasta. It was a little spicy but the prawns were huge and fresh. The cheeseburger and lamb chops were ok.

The kids ordered the Rainbow Paradise and Salted Caramel milkshake at $9.90 each. They came with a huge scoop of ice cream on top. It is fillling and sweet. I think it is a better idea to share.

This place exudes relaxing vibes so its a good place to chill out over a casual meal.

Weekday Lunch Sets are available too.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

review_text:

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Was here for an Edusave award ceremony at the MPH, which is clean and has aircon. The open air area is also sheltered and has a huge fan so it's not hot. There are also counters to perform administrative stuff like collection of vouchers etc. There's also a Starbucks here if you wanna chill. There's a 24/7 fitness here too.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
First time booking a pickleball court for play on Tuesday (20 Jan, 8-9am) and when we arrived at the CC, the only hall that we saw was dark and empty. As the counter was not open, I called the main PA hotline and requested for assistance. The staff tried contacting someone from the CC to no avail. He said he would escalate the matter. When I asked if we should just “cancel” our court instead of continuing to wait, he told me to wait for his update. We left eventually and there was no further callback at all.

We returned to the CC at 1pm and checked with the counter staff, only to find out that the pickleball courts are not located within the CC itself, but at the HDB block opposite, on the 2nd floor. When I informed the staff that there’s no notice indicating that the courts are at a different location, the staff replied that their badminton players all know that the courts are opposite. He even asked if I’m staying in the area.

From booking, to the email acknowledgement, there’s no indication of any information stating that. Even at the CC itself, there are no notices giving directions to the courts. Another staff then came out and added that the information on the poster did not say that the courts are in the hall within the CC itself and that the opposite block is part of Bishan CC.

Totally appalled at the attitude of the counter staff, who seemed to imply that it’s our fault for not knowing where the courts are.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The volunteer staff there were very polite and provided clear, correct guidance. I’m very grateful for their service—it solved my problem and helped me save a considerable amount of money.
Ann Tan

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: It’s a cool place to hangout or wait for a friend to meetup. It’s covered so no issues during rain. There is free water refilling, toilet, and Starbucks too if you need coffee. There’s a lot of exercise activities in the morning for seniors, some activities during the day, and cheering squads use it for practice in the evenings. It’s a bustling place and I am glad to be living nearby and can go there anytime.

review_text: quiet cozy and big library, 4 lvls and a cafe downstairs. Theres a programme rm to study in, which i did, or for u to browse a huge variety of books! borrowed a couple books and had a drink at the cafe. overall it was amazing, plenty of lounge chairs too, if you want a cozy spot to read or work if you cant find a table in the programme room! Librarians were friendly and helped me when i couldn’t locate the toilet! Love this, by far my fav library!!! 🫶

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Clean, new, and convenient MRT train station. It's a great addition to the Upper Thomson neighborhood, especially the many eateries, shopping mall, residential estate, and church. You'll just need to be a little mindful of the exit that you're heading towards, lest you get lost.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟫 Upper Thomson MRT Station/  (TEL) line
___________________________________
🟦 This is a clean, spacious and modern MRT station on the Thomson–East Coast Line.➡️  This was my first visit and I was impressed with how well it connects to the surrounding areas.

🟨 There are 5 Exit points at Upper Thomson MRT station.

🟧 The station exits ( Exit 4) opens up to the charming row of old shophouses along Upper Thomson Road, which are now filled with cafes, dessert spots &  small shops — perfect for exploring.

🔴 Exit 1 ➡️ leads directly into Thomson Plaza ( popular mall), giving commuters sheltered & convenient access to the mall without needing to cross busy roads.

🟩 The station is brightly lit and easy to navigate, making it a pleasant stop for both locals and visitors.
********************************

🟧 Travel Tips:
*******************
🟧  This station sits between Bright Hill and Caldecott stations on the TEL, making it an easy link to the Circle Line at Caldecott Station.

🟧  Several bus services stop nearby along Upper Thomson Road, so it’s convenient to transfer between bus and train.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This MRT Station is TE8 along the brown Thomson East Coast Line (TEL) 🚆🟫 Quite a new and lovely station that is really clean, with excellent infrastructure all around. Plenty of escalators and lifts for those that need this too. Multiple exits with clear directions, with friendly staff to assist if needed. Love that this line connects to the East. Lots of eateries around this station too!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The newly built subway. I bring my little “subway fan” to experience it. The facilities are very complete, bathroom is very clean. Just as good as the other modern MRT station around the island.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The ladies toilet is very dusty. Every time I  enter the toilet, the cubicle door side or top of the door , toilet holder and full areas  are full of dust. I am wondering whether is the Mrt do not have cleaner to clean the toilet ? It is not smelly though.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We would like to express our heartfelt appreciation to the entire team at Mindchamps Bishan for organizing such a memorable concert experience for our child in class K1P 2025.

We could truly see the dedication and hard work the teachers have put in to guide and encourage the children, and to create this valuable exposure for the kids.

A special shout-out to Jiang Laoshi and Ms Sharmila, who have devoted so much time and patience to guide the children, and most importantly, to ensure the kids enjoy the process.

Thank you for creating such wonderful memories for the children. We are truly grateful to be part of this community.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My K2 son has graduated!
The graduation concert was a blast! It was well choreographed and the teachers put in a lot of effort to train the students well to perform their lines. I am genuinely impressed how the students could remember their lines, actions and the sequence in which to do so with their friends, for a 3 hours concert no less. It is very much different from other school's ceremony and very impressive.
Thank you to all the staff of Mindchamps Bishan for the hard work and dedication to make it such an enjoyable concert for the whole family.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: We attended the Family Day Extravaganza organized by Mindchamps Bishan. Although the weather was scorching hot, it did not deter us from having loads of fun. A lot of effort have been put up by the teachers to organize amazing race, game booths, snacks and ice pop bars etc. The kids had a fantastic evening playing and mingling with their classmates as well as watching the fabulous fireworks at the backdrop at an exclusive private area around silver garden.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The teachers and school staff put in a lot of effort to make each event meaningful and enjoyable to the champs and parents.  Thanks Mindchamps Bishan and the entire team letting us enjoy a special moment of togetherness in school with the champs.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
MindChamps @ Bishan has been a wonderful part of our family’s journey. Both of my children have been attending classes there over the past few years, and we’ve been consistently impressed with the experience. We love the genuine care, warmth, and dedication shown by the teachers. They create such a positive, nurturing space where our children feel safe, supported, and genuinely excited to attend school every day.

We’ve seen our kids grow and develop in so many ways, and that speaks volumes about the encouragement and individual attention they’ve received here. They’ve become more confident, curious, and socially engaged, and they’ve truly developed a love for learning that we believe will stay with them for years to come.

We also really appreciate the school’s consistent communication. From regular updates to detailed progress reports, we always feel informed and reassured. The photo updates we receive weekly via the school app are one of the highlights of our week—they give us a heartwarming glimpse into our children’s daily activities and interactions.

We’re truly grateful to the amazing team at MindChamps @ Bishan, and would wholeheartedly recommend them to any parent seeking a nurturing, engaging, and high-quality early childhood education.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
NEX is a big, convenient mall that’s hard to beat for errands or a casual day out. It’s right by Serangoon MRT and the bus interchange, so getting in and out is easy. Inside, it’s bright, clean, and well-air-conditioned; even on weekends when it’s busy, the walkways are wide enough that you can still move comfortably.

Food options are plentiful, everything from quick bites to sit-down spots, and the food court is efficient with staff constantly wiping tables, so turnover is fast. Shopping is just as varied, with fashion, beauty, tech, and a large supermarket, which makes it great for one-stop “get everything done” runs. Families will appreciate the clear signage and clean restrooms, plus plenty of places to take a break if you’re with kids or older folks. Overall, a reliable place to eat, shop, and cool off, practical for a lunch stop and pleasant enough for an unhurried weekend stroll.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Huge shopping mall connected to Serangoon MRT. Has all outlets that you need for shopping and food.
Very accessible. Can get a bit crowded on weekends.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Probably the busiest, most bustling mall in the heartlands. It’s a decent size with a pretty good tenant mix, but word on the street is that the old Isetan spot is being turned into a bunch of boring tuition centres and a Kiztopia. If that’s true, we’ll probably be visiting a lot less.

There’s also been a massive proliferation of PRC restaurants in recent years, which makes us even less inclined to frequent the place. We might drop by when Chin Mee Chin finally opens, but we aren’t going to visit as much as before.

P.S The elevators are always under maintenance or not working.

review_text:
Good Location Shopping!!!
But saw such behaviours of youngsters is totally embarrassing and flabergusted, giving others no place to walk via bulldozing in a row.
Saw them few times doing this in 2025 and 2 times in 2026. Video was captured when I changed my handphone and was trying out it's functions.
The worst is, this group have been doing this bulldozing walks everywhere they go, even Tourists has been complaining on TikTok regarding such behaviours when they visited Singapore.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The management of NEX mall (Serangoon MRT) must be fast asleep. A couple of their escalators (to L2 & B1, see photos) have been out of service for quite a few months and no repair work has been initiated. These escalators are covered with cladding and nobody has been spotted working on them! Rather than have them fixed, signs are instead posted to direct customers to other (working, for the time being) escalators. This smacks of apathy and a pathetic lack of professional pride & customer care. Whoever is at the top needs to deal with it - unless, of course, he/she/they also can’t be bothered. In which case, the problem is endemic.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

review_text:
Timbre+ One North is a great dining destination in Singapore's One North district, offering a vibrant atmosphere that appeals to a wide range of visitors. Whether you are meeting colleagues after work, catching up with friends, hosting a casual business discussion, or simply looking for a good meal, it provides a welcoming and comfortable setting.

One of its biggest strengths is the variety of food available. With options ranging from Indian and Chinese cuisine to continental dishes and local favourites, there is something to suit almost every taste and preference. This makes it an excellent choice for groups with diverse food interests.

The venue is clean, well organized, and efficiently managed, creating a pleasant dining experience even during busy periods. The lively yet relaxed environment makes it suitable for both working lunches and social gatherings.

Good food, reasonable prices, and a wide selection of cuisines make Timbre+ One North a place that offers excellent value for money and is well worth a visit when in the area.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
A 24/7 Shopping Paradise with Great Prices! 🛒🇸🇬
Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Mustafa Centre is one of the most famous shopping malls in Singapore, and for a good reason. It’s open 24/7, making it incredibly convenient for anyone to shop at any time of the day or night.
Why I recommend it:
• Best Prices: Almost all goods are sold at very reasonable and competitive prices.
• One-Stop Shop: From electronics and jewelry to groceries and souvenirs, you can find everything here. It’s the go-to spot for foreigners and migrant workers to buy gifts and essentials for their families back home before vacation.
Important Tip for Shoppers:
I highly recommend checking the expiry dates on all food and consumable items before you buy. Try to ensure they have at least one year of validity.
My Experience: I’ve had issues multiple times where I bought items in bulk, but since the expiry date was too close, I couldn't finish them in time. This is a big waste of money, so please double-check your basket before heading to the cashier!
#MustafaCentre #SingaporeShopping
#LittleIndia
#TravelTips
#BudgetShopping #SmartShopping

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Mustafa Centre is one of the most unique shopping experiences in Singapore. Located in the heart of Little India, this massive multi-level store literally has everything — from electronics and perfumes to groceries, souvenirs, jewelry, clothing, and even travel essentials.
What makes it even more impressive is that it’s open 24 hours a day. Whether you need something late at night or just enjoy browsing without crowds during off-peak hours, it’s incredibly convenient.
The store can feel a bit overwhelming at first because it’s packed floor-to-ceiling with products and narrow aisles, but that’s part of its charm. Prices are competitive, and you can often find great deals, especially on chocolates, cosmetics, and electronics.
If you’re staying in the Little India area or nearby, it’s definitely worth a visit — even if just to see how massive and diverse the selection is. A true Singapore experience for shoppers.

We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Saw a poster at the MRT station at Outram Park 🚇 and spent a few minutes reading it. It was very touching. Jia you, Junle!

Not sure if the story is real or not, but I hope to see more posters like this everywhere — stories about the elderly, parents, couples, and children. Stories that remind us of love, family, and life. ❤️

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Caldecott MRT station is one of the calmer, cleaner stations I’ve used, and it’s honestly a nice change from the usual crowded ones. The place is bright, tidy, and easy to move around in. The layout is simple, the signs are clear, and the platforms feel spacious instead of cramped. Even during busier hours, it doesn’t get overwhelming.

One thing that really stands out is the staff. They’re friendly and actually helpful — not the kind who just point vaguely and carry on. If you’re unsure about directions or have a card issue, they take the time to explain things properly without rushing you. I’ve seen them step forward to assist people who look confused, which is something not every station does.

Escalators and lifts are usually in good condition, trains come regularly, and the whole station has a very calm, organised feel. It’s not flashy or loud, but everything runs smoothly, and the people working there make the experience even better.

Overall, Caldecott is a peaceful, well-maintained station with staff who genuinely care about doing their job well. Great spot for a stress-free commute.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟧Caldecott Station ⬛️ Exit 4 (✅️ I always wait here for Feeder Bus 235 to bring me home to Toa Payoh East).

😁 As of early 2023, I've fully mastered knowing all the passageways, exits, entrances of the escalators etc of Caldecott Station. ✳️ I will not be lost again.

🟧 In the beginning, I was a tad confused when the Thomson-East Coast Line opened at Caldecott Station and more passageways, exits, etc ,all opened up. 🙏🏻✔️But now, I am okay. Not confused anymore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
At Outram Park MRT station, especially with my family. I were there with my parents to support my brother for his appointments at Singapore General Hospital (SGH) and the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS). It's a testament to how well the SGH Campus is integrated with the public transport network, making what could be a stressful day a little bit easier.

I loved going to Outram Park MRT Station (EW16) today because of how convenient it was. The two photo updates I shared perfectly illustrate why this station is so helpful. The "Exit Directory" is a lifesaver, clearly listing all the major institutions within the SGH Campus. I can see at a glance that both the National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS) and Singapore General Hospital (SGH) are accessible via Exit 6 and Exit 7.

The second photo, the "Locality Map," reinforces this. It shows a detailed layout of the entire SGH Campus, making it easy to see the direct, sheltered walking paths from the MRT station to the various hospitals. It's a well-designed system that ensures visitors and patients can navigate the area with ease, even if they are unfamiliar with the layout.

With my brother's checkup at National Heart Centre Singapore (NHCS) today and his appointment at Singapore General Hospital (SGH) yesterday, the convenient access from Outram Park MRT must have made a big difference for my family. The clear signage and well-laid-out campus truly minimize the hassle of getting around, allowing my family to focus on what's most important: my brother's care.

In short, my experience was great because Outram Park MRT station and the SGH Campus are a perfect example of a well-planned and user-friendly system, where every detail is considered to make a potentially difficult journey as smooth as possible.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Conveniently located in Toa Payoh rise with a few bus connections (3 bus connections from Toa Payoh town) and a well connected mrt infrastructure! Travelling to different parts of the island via public transport, such as the various bus services, Circle Line and Thomson East Coast Line has been more convenient than ever!
